sections.strategy: The April buying/merchandising restructure of the Tu team remains in consultation with no confirmed resolution this week; trade press expects role cuts alongside new role creation as Sainsbury's simplifies operations in the final year of its Next Level Strategy. Q1 context (16 weeks to 20 June) still frames the picture: Tu sales down 2.1% but outperforming a soft clothing market and taking share on improved availability and style credentials, while General Merchandise fell 6.3% as space shifts to food.
sections.pricing: Tu's fourth-year school uniform price lock (held at 2022 prices, items from £3, two full uniforms from £14) is the week's value story, amplified by a limited-time 20% off uniform and footwear promotion. Independent basket comparisons this summer named Tu the cheapest full uniform (£47), ahead of Morrisons, George at Asda and Tesco F&F — sharpening its back-to-school positioning against all key rivals.
sections.launches: High summer 2026 womenswear drops continue landing through August: relaxed silhouettes, balloon-leg trousers, jorts, 100% linen, cotton poplin, crochet and suede, with print use up fivefold. 80% of the range is £25 or less and 92% of swimwear £20 or under, sold via tu.co.uk, Argos and selected supermarkets. Back-to-school uniform (200+ styles, 100 shoe styles) is fully live in stores and online.
sections.gossip: Post-Q1 analyst commentary remains net positive on Tu despite the -2.1% print, framed as share gains in a declining market. Trade press framing is split — Just Style ran 'Tu clothing sales tumble' while Drapers emphasised outperformance — and consumer press (Hello!, money-saving sites) is pushing Tu uniform as the best-value back-to-school buy, driving reported parent 'rushes' on the range.
soWhat: The unresolved buying/merchandising restructure means Tu's supplier-facing contacts may still be in flux — confirm buyer/merchandiser continuity before committing SS27 negotiations.
A fourth-year uniform price freeze plus 20% promo signals sustained cost pressure on basics; expect firm FOB negotiation on poly-cotton uniform staples despite high back-to-school volumes.

Tu winning share in a soft market while GM space shrinks points to open-to-buy concentrating in fewer, fabric-led clothing lines (linen, poplin, texture) — differentiated fabric capability is where new Tu business is being won.
